paragraphs.text:
SRD-46 reports **only two Cu(II)–glycine aqueous stability constants** for this system: the cumulative constants for **CuGly** and **CuGly₂**.

| Species | β definition | Equation |
|---|---|---|
| ML | β₁ = [ML]/([M][L]) | Cu²⁺ + Gly⁻ ⇌ CuGly |
| ML₂ | β₂ = [ML₂]/([M][L]²) | Cu²⁺ + 2 Gly⁻ ⇌ CuGly₂ |

claims.text: SRD-46 reports **only two Cu(II)–glycine aqueous stability constants** for this system

paragraphs.text:
### What’s in SRD-46 for this system
| Finding | Result |
|---|---|
| Total measurements | 30 |
| Stoichiometries found | ML, ML₂ only |
| Reference equilibrium networks | 9 |
| Protonated / hydroxo / polynuclear Cu(II)–glycine species | None found for metal_41 + ligand_5760 in this dataset |

A quick chemistry note: the **1:2 complex is much more stable overall** than the 1:1 complex, as expected for Cu(II) binding to the amino acid donor set of glycinate. The data also show some dependence on temperature and ionic strength, so comparisons should be made only at matched conditions.

**Source:** NIST SRD-46, Cu²⁺ (`metal_41`) + glycine / aminoacetic acid (`ligand_5760`).
